sections.paragraph:
fut. יִלְפֹּת i.q. Arabic لفت to bend, to incline something, Jud. 16:29 .

Niphal, to bend oneself-(a) to turn aside from the way. Jud. 6:18 , יִלָּֽפְתוּ אָרְתֹות דַּרְבָּם “the journeyers of their way turn aside,” i.e. those who journey that way; die Wanderer, die des Weges kommen.-(b) to turn oneself back to see (Arab. Conj. I. V. VIII. id.), Rth 3:8.
